prs.title: fix: handle grepFilterSpecs as string "true" from CLI environment variables
prs.body:
<!-- Thanks for contributing! PLEASE:
- Read the contributing guidelines: https://github.com/cypress-io/cypress/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Mark this PR as Draft if not ready for review.
-->

- Closes #32676

### Additional details
Fix `grepFilterSpecs` CLI env variable issue. Previously, passing `--env grepFilterSpecs=true` failed because env vars are strings and only boolean `true` was checked.

Changes:
- `npm/grep/src/plugin.ts`: handle boolean `true` and string `"true"` (case-insensitive)
- `npm/grep/test/unit.spec.ts`: added tests for `true`, `"true"`, `"TRUE"`, `"True"`, `false`, `"false"`, and `undefined`
- `npm/grep/README.md`: clarified CLI usage

### Steps to test
1. Run unit tests:
```bash
npm test

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Accepts grepFilterSpecs as the string "true" (any case) in the plugin and adds unit tests covering boolean/string values and undefined.
> 
> - **Grep Plugin (`npm/grep/src/plugin.ts`)**
>   - Parse `env.grepFilterSpecs` as enabled when boolean `true` or string `'true'` (case-insensitive).
> - **Tests (`npm/grep/test/unit.spec.ts`)**
>   - Add `plugin` tests validating `grepFilterSpecs` handling for `true`, `'true'`, `'TRUE'`, `'True'`, `false`, `'false'`, and `undefined`.
>   - Minor test import adjustments.
>

prs.title: fix: fireEvent.mouseEnter not forwarding relatedTarget properly
prs.body:
Fixes #1422

## Problem
`fireEvent.mouseEnter` was not forwarding `relatedTarget` properly - it would be `window` instead of the specified element.

## Solution  
Changed spread operator to explicit parameters in `fire-event.js`:
- `(...args) =>` → `(node, init) =>`

This ensures `relatedTarget` and other event properties are properly passed through.

## Changes
- Fixed `mouseEnter`, `mouseLeave`, `pointerEnter`, `pointerLeave`, `blur`, `focus`
- Added comprehensive tests

## Testing
```js
fireEvent.mouseEnter(element, { relatedTarget: mockElement })
// Now correctly receives mockElement instead of window

prs.title: POC: Comprehensive WebSocket implementation gaps analysis
prs.body:
Description:

This PR provides a comprehensive analysis of GoFr's WebSocket implementation, identifying 10 critical gaps that prevent production use in real-time applications. It includes a complete demonstration project showing current capabilities and limitations, plus a technical roadmap for improvements.

Key Deliverables:

Gap analysis document with 10 identified limitations and priority classification

Live demonstration project (examples/websocket-gaps-demo/) with working examples and test cases

Foundation code for future improvements (message type constants)

Critical Gaps Found:

No broadcasting mechanism for multiple clients

Missing connection lifecycle management

No health monitoring (ping/pong)

Zero WebSocket metrics

Limited to text messages only

No authentication middleware

Impact: Transforms WebSocket implementation from demo-level to production-ready for chat, notifications, and real-time applications.

Addresses #2578

Breaking Changes (if applicable):

None. Adds new analysis and demonstration code without modifying existing functionality.

Additional Information:

All code uses existing GoFr APIs to demonstrate current state

Test suite validates each identified gap

Provides foundation for 10 individual follow-up issues

Checklist:

 I have formatted my code using goimport and golangci-lint.
 All new code is covered by unit tests.
 This PR does not decrease the overall code coverage.
 I have reviewed the code comments and documentation for clarity.

prs.title: fix: Add N8N_PROTOCOL=https to n8n templates to fix Telegram webhook …
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes n8n Telegram webhook registration failures by adding the missing `N8N_PROTOCOL=https` environment variable to all n8n templates.

## Changes
- Added `N8N_PROTOCOL=https` environment variable to `templates/compose/n8n.yaml`
- Added `N8N_PROTOCOL=https` environment variable to `templates/compose/n8n-with-postgresql.yaml`
- Added `N8N_PROTOCOL=https` environment variable to `templates/compose/n8n-with-postgres-and-worker.yaml`

## Issues
Fixes #7308

## Problem
n8n Telegram webhooks fail with "Bad Request: bad webhook: Failed to resolve host: Temporary failure in name resolution" when deployed via Coolify without proper protocol configuration.

## Solution
The `N8N_PROTOCOL=https` environment variable tells n8n to use HTTPS protocol for webhook URLs when running behind a reverse proxy, which is required for Coolify deployments.

prs.title: fix(rsc): normalize double slashes in manifest URLs
prs.body:
Fixes #14583 by using joinPaths utility to normalize double slashes in getManifestUrl function, preventing ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED errors




## Summary
Fixes #14583 - RSC routing issue with double slashes causing ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED errors.

## Problem
URLs with double slashes (e.g., `//en//test2/test`) caused malformed manifest URLs like `https://en//test/test.manifest`, leading to network errors in RSC applications.

## Solution
- Import `joinPaths` utility in `browser.tsx`
- Normalize paths in `getManifestUrl` function for:
  - Single path manifest URLs  
  - Multiple paths in query params
  - Basename configurations

## Changes
- **packages/react-router/lib/rsc/browser.tsx**: Added `joinPaths` import and normalized paths in `getManifestUrl`
- **packages/react-router/__tests__/rsc-double-slashes-test.ts**: Unit tests for URL normalization
- **integration/rsc-double-slashes-test.ts**: Integration tests for RSC functionality

## Testing
✅ Single path normalization: `//en//test2/test` → `/en/test2/test.manifest`
✅ Multiple paths normalization in query params
✅ Basename normalization
✅ Backward compatibility with normal URLs

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)

Closes #14583
